   677→def _save_candidates_to_db(db_manager, candidates: List[CandidateBasic], source_search: str) -> None:
   678→    """
   679→    Save or update candidates in database.
   680→
   681→    Args:
   682→        db_manager: Database manager instance
   683→        candidates: List of candidates to save
   684→        source_search: Search keywords that found these candidates
   685→    """
   686→    try:
   687→        with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   688→            # Batch pre-fetch existing candidates to avoid N+1 queries
   689→            maimai_ids = [c.maimai_id for c in candidates]
   690→            existing_by_id = {
   691→                row.maimai_id: row
   692→                for row in session.query(Candidate).filter(
   693→                    Candidate.maimai_id.in_(maimai_ids)
   694→                ).all()
   695→            }
   696→
   697→            # Also batch-fetch by name+company for dedup
   698→            name_company_pairs = [(c.name, c.company) for c in candidates]
   699→            from sqlalchemy import tuple_
   700→            existing_by_name = {}
   701→            if name_company_pairs:
   702→                for row in session.query(Candidate).filter(
   703→                    tuple_(Candidate.name, Candidate.company).in_(name_company_pairs)
   704→                ).all():
   705→                    existing_by_name[(row.name, row.company)] = row
   706→
   707→            for c in candidates:
   708→                existing = existing_by_id.get(c.maimai_id)
   709→                if not existing:
   710→                    existing = existing_by_name.get((c.name, c.company))
   711→
   712→                if existing:
   713→                    existing.name = c.name
   714→                    existing.title = c.title
   715→                    existing.company = c.company
   716→                    existing.location = c.location
   717→                    existing.profile_url = c.profile_url
   718→                else:
   719→                    candidate = Candidate(
   720→                        maimai_id=c.maimai_id,
   721→                        name=c.name,
   722→                        title=c.title,
   723→                        company=c.company,
   724→                        location=c.location,
   725→                        profile_url=c.profile_url,
   726→                        source_search=source_search
   727→                    )
   728→                    session.add(candidate)
   729→
   730→            logger.debug(f"Saved {len(candidates)} candidates to database")
   731→
   732→    except Exception as e:
   733→        logger.error(f"Failed to save candidates to database: {e}")
   734→        raise
   735→
   736→
   737→def _log_search_history(
   738→    db_manager,
   739→    keywords: str,
   740→    company: Optional[tuple],
   741→    location: Optional[str],
   742→    pages: int,
   743→    results_count: int
   744→) -> None:
   745→    """Log search to history table."""
   746→    try:
   747→        with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   748→            history = SearchHistory(
   749→                criteria={
   750→                    'keywords': keywords,
   751→                    'company': list(company) if company else None,
   752→                    'location': location,
   753→                    'pages': pages
   754→                },
   755→                results_count=results_count
   756→            )
   757→            session.add(history)
   758→    except Exception as e:
   759→        logger.error(f"Failed to log search history: {e}")
   760→
   761→
   762→async def _click_avatar_for_profile_url(page: Page, name: str, company: str) -> Optional[str]:
   763→    """Try to extract profile URL for a candidate from the DOM contactcard pattern.
   764→
   765→    Searches the current page for a contactcard element whose parent card
   766→    contains the candidate's name (and optionally company), then constructs
   767→    the profile URL from the extracted user ID.
   768→
   769→    Args:
   770→        page: Playwright page on the search results
   771→        name: Candidate name to match
   772→        company: Candidate company to help disambiguate
   773→
   774→    Returns:
   775→        Profile URL string, or None if not found
   776→    """
   777→    try:
   778→        user_id = await page.evaluate('''(args) => {
   779→            const { name, company } = args;
   780→            // Check contactcard_{numeric_id} ancestors
   781→            const ccElements = document.querySelectorAll('[id^="contactcard_"]');
   782→            for (const el of ccElements) {
   783→                const text = el.textContent || '';
   784→                if (!text.includes(name)) continue;
   785→                if (company && !text.includes(company)) continue;
   786→                const match = el.id.match(/^contactcard_(\\d+)$/);
   787→                if (match) return match[1];
   788→            }
   789→            // Check member_entry_{numeric_id} descendants
   790→            const meElements = document.querySelectorAll('[id^="member_entry_"]');
   791→            for (const el of meElements) {
   792→                // Walk up to find the card containing this member_entry
   793→                let card = el.parentElement;
   794→                for (let i = 0; i < 8 && card; i++) {
   795→                    const text = card.textContent || '';
   796→                    if (text.includes(name) && (!company || text.includes(company))) {
   797→                        const match = el.id.match(/^member_entry_(\\d+)$/);
   798→                        if (match) return match[1];
   799→                    }
   800→                    card = card.parentElement;
   801→                }
   802→            }
   803→            return null;
   804→        }''', {'name': name, 'company': company})
   805→
   806→        if user_id:
   807→            profile_url = f"https://maimai.cn/contact/detail/{user_id}?dstu={user_id}"
   808→            logger.info(f"Resolved profile_url for '{name}' via contactcard: {profile_url}")
   809→            return profile_url
   810→
   811→        logger.debug(f"No contactcard found for '{name}'")
   812→        return None
   813→
   814→    except Exception as e:
   815→        logger.debug(f"_click_avatar_for_profile_url error for '{name}': {e}")
   816→        return None
   817→
   818→
   819→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   820→# Enterprise recruitment path (Path B)
   821→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   822→
   823→MAIMAI_HOME_URL = "https://maimai.cn"
   824→ENTERPRISE_URL_MARKER = "ent/talents/discover"
   825→
   826→
   827→async def search_candidates_enterprise(
   828→    keywords: str,
   829→    company: Optional[tuple] = None,
   830→    pages: int = 1,
   831→    browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None,
   832→    keep_tab_open: bool = False,
   833→) -> List[CandidateBasic]:
   834→    """Search for candidates via the enterprise recruitment path.
   835→
   836→    Flow:
   837→    1. Switch to primary tab -> navigate to homepage -> click "招聘" tab (opens new tab)
   838→    2. Wait for enterprise page to load
   839→    3. Add keyword filter -> submit
   840→    4. Optionally add company filter
   841→    5. Extract candidates from enterprise card layout
   842→    6. For additional pages, scroll to trigger infinite scroll
   843→    7. Close enterprise tab unless keep_tab_open is True
   844→
   845→    Args:
   846→        keywords: Search keywords
   847→        company: Filter by company names (optional)
   848→        pages: Number of scroll-pages to extract
   849→        browser: Browser manager instance
   850→        keep_tab_open: If True, leave enterprise tab open (for inline messaging)
   851→
   852→    Returns:
   853→        List of CandidateBasic objects
   854→    """
   855→    validate_keywords(keywords)
   856→
   857→    if browser is None:
   858→        browser = get_browser_manager()
   859→
   860→    config = get_config()
   861→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
   862→    db_manager = get_db_manager()
   863→
   864→    await ensure_logged_in(browser)
   865→
   866→    all_candidates: List[CandidateBasic] = []
   867→    enterprise_tab_opened = False
   868→
   869→    with Progress(
   870→        SpinnerColumn(),
   871→        TextColumn("[progress.description]{task.description}"),
   872→        console=console,
   873→    ) as progress:
   874→        task = progress.add_task("Opening enterprise recruitment...", total=None)
   875→
   876→        try:
   877→            await async_check_rate_limit("search")
   878→
   879→            # Always start from the primary tab to avoid navigating user's open content
   880→            browser.switch_to_primary_tab()
   881→            page = browser.page
   882→
   883→            # Step 1: Navigate to homepage and click 招聘 tab
   884→            await retry_navigation(page, MAIMAI_HOME_URL, wait_until="domcontentloaded")
   885→            await async_random_delay("page_load")
   886→
   887→            sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.recruitment_tab")
   888→            recruitment_tab = await page.wait_for_selector(sel, timeout=5000)
   889→            if not recruitment_tab:
   890→                raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Could not find recruitment tab: {sel}")
   891→
   892→            # Click opens a new tab — use BrowserManager API
   893→            await recruitment_tab.click()
   894→            page = await browser.wait_for_new_tab(timeout=15000)
   895→            enterprise_tab_opened = True
   896→
   897→            progress.update(task, description="Enterprise page loaded")
   898→
   899→            # Wait for the enterprise page URL
   900→            try:
   901→                await page.wait_for_url(
   902→                    lambda url: ENTERPRISE_URL_MARKER in url,
   903→                    timeout=15000,
   904→                )
   905→            except Exception:
   906→                logger.warning(f"Enterprise URL marker not found, current URL: {page.url}")
   907→
   908→            await async_random_delay("action")
   909→
   910→            # Step 2: Add keyword
   911→            progress.update(task, description=f"Adding keyword: {keywords}")
   912→
   913→            add_kw_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.add_keyword_button")
   914→            add_kw_btn = await page.wait_for_selector(add_kw_sel, timeout=5000)
   915→            if add_kw_btn:
   916→                await add_kw_btn.click()
   917→                await async_random_delay("action")
   918→
   919→            kw_input_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.keyword_input")
   920→            await page.wait_for_selector(kw_input_sel, timeout=5000)
   921→            await human_type(page, kw_input_sel, keywords)
   922→            await async_random_delay("action")
   923→
   924→            kw_submit_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.keyword_submit")
   925→            submit_btn = await page.wait_for_selector(kw_submit_sel, timeout=3000)
   926→            if submit_btn:
   927→                await submit_btn.click()
   928→                await async_random_delay("page_load")
   929→
   930→            # Step 3: Add company filter if specified
   931→            if company:
   932→                progress.update(task, description="Adding company filter...")
   933→                add_co_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.add_company_button")
   934→                add_co_btn = await page.wait_for_selector(add_co_sel, timeout=5000)
   935→                if add_co_btn:
   936→                    await add_co_btn.click()
   937→                    await async_random_delay("action")
   938→
   939→                co_input_sel = config.get_selector("enterprise.company_input")
   940→                for co_name in company:
   941→                    await page.wait_for_selector(co_input_sel, timeout=5000)
   942→                    await human_type(page, co_input_sel, co_name)
   943→                    await async_random_delay("action")
   944→
   945→                    # Click suggestion dropdown item
   946→                    try:
   947→                        suggestion = await page.wait_for_selector(
   948→                            f"text='{co_name}'", timeout=3000
   949→                        )
   950→                        if suggestion:
   951→                            await suggestion.click()
   952→                            await async_random_delay("action")
   953→                    except Exception:
   954→                        logger.debug(f"Company suggestion not found for: {co_name}")
   955→
   956→            # Step 4: Wait for results and extract
   957→            await page.wait_for_timeout(3000)
   958→            await check_captcha(page, report_to_rate_limiter=True)
   959→
   960→            for page_num in range(1, pages + 1):
   961→                progress.update(task, description=f"Extracting page {page_num}/{pages}...")
   962→
   963→                candidates = await _extract_enterprise_results(page)
   964→
   965→                if not candidates:
   966→                    logger.info(f"No enterprise results on page {page_num}")
   967→                    break
   968→
   969→                # Filter non-R&D roles
   970→                before_filter = len(candidates)
   971→                candidates = [c for c in candidates if not _is_non_rd_title(c.title)]
   972→                filtered = before_filter - len(candidates)
   973→                if filtered > 0:
   974→                    logger.info(f"Filtered out {filtered} non-R&D candidates")
   975→
   976→                all_candidates.extend(candidates)
   977→                progress.update(task, description=f"Page {page_num}: Found {len(candidates)} candidates")
   978→
   979→                rate_limiter.log_action("search", {
   980→                    "keywords": keywords,
   981→                    "page": page_num,
   982→                    "results": len(candidates),
   983→                    "mode": "enterprise",
   984→                })
   985→
   986→                try:
   987→                    _save_candidates_to_db(db_manager, candidates, keywords)
   988→                except Exception as e:
   989→                    console.print(f"[yellow]Warning: Failed to save candidates to DB: {e}[/yellow]")
   990→
   991→                # Scroll for next page (infinite scroll)
   992→                if page_num < pages:
   993→                    await human_scroll(page, "down", distance=800)
   994→                    await async_random_delay("page_load")
   995→                    await page.wait_for_timeout(2000)
   996→
   997→        except Exception as e:
   998→            logger.error(f"Enterprise search error: {e}")
   999→            await browser.screenshot("enterprise_search_error")
  1000→            progress.update(task, description=f"Error: {str(e)[:40]}")
  1001→        finally:
  1002→            # Close the enterprise tab unless caller wants it open for inline messaging
  1003→            if enterprise_tab_opened and not keep_tab_open:
  1004→                try:
  1005→                    await browser.close_current_tab()
  1006→                except Exception:
  1007→                    pass  # Best-effort cleanup; tab may already be closed
  1008→
  1009→    _log_search_history(db_manager, keywords, company, None, pages, len(all_candidates))
  1010→
  1011→    console.print(f"[green]Enterprise search complete. Found {len(all_candidates)} candidates.[/green]")
  1012→    return all_candidates
  1013→
  1014→
  1015→async def _extract_enterprise_results(page: Page) -> List[CandidateBasic]:
  1016→    """Extract candidate information from enterprise recruitment results page.
  1017→
  1018→    The enterprise UI has a different card layout from search_center.
  1019→    Uses JS-based extraction tailored to the enterprise talent cards.
  1020→
  1021→    Args:
  1022→        page: Playwright page instance on the enterprise results page
  1023→
  1024→    Returns:
  1025→        List of CandidateBasic objects
  1026→    """
  1027→    candidates = []
  1028→
  1029→    try:
  1030→        await page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle", timeout=10000)
  1031→    except Exception:
  1032→        pass  # Continue even if networkidle times out
  1033→
  1034→    try:
  1035→        candidates_data = await page.evaluate('''() => {
  1036→            const candidates = [];
  1037→            const seenIds = new Set();
  1038→
  1039→            // Enterprise talent cards typically have a structured layout
  1040→            // Try multiple selectors for the card containers
  1041→            const cardSelectors = [
  1042→                '[class*="talent-card"]',
  1043→                '[class*="candidate-card"]',
  1044→                '[class*="recommend-card"]',
  1045→                '[class*="talent-item"]',
  1046→                '[class*="card-wrap"]',
  1047→            ];
  1048→
  1049→            let cards = [];
  1050→            for (const sel of cardSelectors) {
  1051→                cards = document.querySelectorAll(sel);
  1052→                if (cards.length > 0) break;
  1053→            }
  1054→
  1055→            // Fallback: find cards by avatar image pattern (same as search_center)
  1056→            if (cards.length === 0) {
  1057→                const images = document.querySelectorAll('img');
  1058→                const foundCards = [];
  1059→
  1060→                for (const img of images) {
  1061→                    const rect = img.getBoundingClientRect();
  1062→                    if (rect.width > 100 || rect.height > 100) continue;
  1063→                    if (rect.width < 30 || rect.height < 30) continue;
  1064→
  1065→                    let card = img.parentElement;
  1066→                    for (let i = 0; i < 8 && card; i++) {
  1067→                        const cardRect = card.getBoundingClientRect();
  1068→                        if (cardRect.width > 200 && cardRect.height > 80) {
  1069→                            break;
  1070→                        }
  1071→                        card = card.parentElement;
  1072→                    }
  1073→                    if (card) foundCards.push(card);
  1074→                }
  1075→                cards = foundCards;
  1076→            }
  1077→
  1078→            for (const card of cards) {
  1079→                const textParts = [];
  1080→                const walker = document.createTreeWalker(card, NodeFilter.SHOW_TEXT, null, false);
  1081→                while (walker.nextNode()) {
  1082→                    const text = walker.currentNode.textContent.trim();
  1083→                    if (text && text.length > 0 && text.length < 100) {
  1084→                        textParts.push(text);
  1085→                    }
  1086→                }
  1087→
  1088→                let name = '';
  1089→                let company = '';
  1090→                let title = '';
  1091→
  1092→                const skipPatterns = [
  1093→                    /^立即沟通$/,
  1094→                    /^不合适$/,
  1095→                    /^感兴趣$/,
  1096→                    /^不感兴趣$/,
  1097→                    /^(\\d+\\s*)?年/,
  1098→                    /^[|｜·]$/,
  1099→                    /^在线|^离线|^活跃/,
  1100→                ];
  1101→
  1102→                const isSkippable = (text) => {
  1103→                    return skipPatterns.some(p => p.test(text));
  1104→                };
  1105→
  1106→                for (let i = 0; i < textParts.length; i++) {
  1107→                    const part = textParts[i];
  1108→                    if (isSkippable(part)) continue;
  1109→
  1110→                    if (!name && part.length >= 2 && part.length <= 15) {
  1111→                        if (/^[\\u4e00-\\u9fa5a-zA-Z·\\s]+$/.test(part)) {
  1112→                            name = part;
  1113→                            continue;
  1114→                        }
  1115→                    }
  1116→
  1117→                    if (name && !company && part.length > 1) {
  1118→                        company = part;
  1119→                        continue;
  1120→                    }
  1121→
  1122→                    if (name && company && !title && part.length > 1) {
  1123→                        title = part;
  1124→                        break;
  1125→                    }
  1126→                }
  1127→
  1128→                if (!name) continue;
  1129→
  1130→                const idSource = (name || '') + '|' + (company || '') + '|' + (title || '');
  1131→                let hash = 0;
  1132→                for (let j = 0; j < idSource.length; j++) {
  1133→                    const ch = idSource.charCodeAt(j);
  1134→                    hash = ((hash << 5) - hash) + ch;
  1135→                    hash = hash & hash;
  1136→                }
  1137→                const maimaiId = 'mm_' + Math.abs(hash).toString(16).padStart(8, '0');
  1138→
  1139→                if (seenIds.has(maimaiId)) continue;
  1140→                seenIds.add(maimaiId);
  1141→
  1142→                // Try to extract profile URL
  1143→                let profileUrl = '';
  1144→                const links = card.querySelectorAll('a[href]');
  1145→                for (const link of links) {
  1146→                    const href = link.getAttribute('href') || '';
  1147→                    if (href.includes('/profile/detail') || href.includes('/contact/detail/') || href.includes('dstu=') || href.includes('egid=')) {
  1148→                        profileUrl = href.startsWith('http') ? href : 'https://maimai.cn' + href;
  1149→                        break;
  1150→                    }
  1151→                }
  1152→
  1153→                candidates.push({
  1154→                    name: name,
  1155→                    title: title || '',
  1156→                    company: company || '',
  1157→                    maimai_id: maimaiId,
  1158→                    profile_url: profileUrl,
  1159→                    location: '',
  1160→                });
  1161→            }
  1162→
  1163→            return candidates;
  1164→        }''')
  1165→
  1166→        logger.info(f"Enterprise extraction found {len(candidates_data)} candidates")
  1167→
  1168→        for data in candidates_data:
  1169→            if data.get("name"):
  1170→                candidates.append(CandidateBasic(
  1171→                    maimai_id=data.get("maimai_id", ""),
  1172→                    name=data.get("name", ""),
  1173→                    title=data.get("title", ""),
  1174→                    company=data.get("company", ""),
  1175→                    location=data.get("location", ""),
  1176→                    profile_url=data.get("profile_url", ""),
  1177→                ))
  1178→
  1179→        # Filter out premium placeholders
  1180→        candidates = [c for c in candidates if c.name not in PREMIUM_PLACEHOLDER_NAMES]
  1181→
  1182→        logger.info(f"Enterprise: extracted {len(candidates)} candidates from page")
  1183→
  1184→    except Exception as e:
  1185→        logger.error(f"Error extracting enterprise results: {e}")
  1186→
  1187→    return candidates
  1188→

1→"""Database models and setup using SQLAlchemy."""
     2→
     3→from contextlib import contextmanager
     4→from datetime import datetime, timezone
     5→from pathlib import Path
     6→from typing import Optional
     7→
     8→from sqlalchemy import (
     9→    Column, Integer, String, Text, DateTime, JSON,
    10→    ForeignKey, create_engine, Index, CheckConstraint
    11→)
    12→from sqlalchemy.orm import declarative_base, sessionmaker, relationship
    13→from sqlalchemy.pool import StaticPool
    14→from loguru import logger
    15→
    16→from src.utils.config import get_config
    17→
    18→Base = declarative_base()
    19→
    20→
    21→def _utcnow():
    22→    """Return timezone-aware UTC now (replaces deprecated datetime.utcnow)."""
    23→    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    24→
    25→
    26→class Candidate(Base):
    27→    """Candidate model - stores candidate profile information."""
    28→
    29→    __tablename__ = 'candidates'
    30→
    31→    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    32→    maimai_id = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False, index=True)
    33→    name = Column(String(100))
    34→    title = Column(String(200))
    35→    company = Column(String(200))
    36→    location = Column(String(100))
    37→    profile_url = Column(Text)
    38→
    39→    # JSON fields for structured data
    40→    basic_info = Column(JSON)
    41→    education = Column(JSON)
    42→    work_experience = Column(JSON)
    43→    skills = Column(JSON)
    44→
    45→    # Metadata
    46→    first_seen = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow)
    47→    last_updated = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, onupdate=_utcnow)
    48→    source_search = Column(Text)  # Search keywords that found this candidate
    49→
    50→    # Relationships
    51→    messages = relationship("Message", back_populates="candidate")
    52→
    53→    def __repr__(self):
    54→        return f"<Candidate(maimai_id='{self.maimai_id}', name='{self.name}', company='{self.company}')>"
    55→
    56→
    57→class Message(Base):
    58→    """Message model - stores sent messages to candidates."""
    59→
    60→    __tablename__ = 'messages'
    61→
    62→    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    63→    candidate_maimai_id = Column(String(50), ForeignKey('candidates.maimai_id'), index=True)
    64→    template_name = Column(String(100))
    65→    content = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    66→    status = Column(String(20), CheckConstraint("status IN ('sent', 'failed', 'cancelled')"))
    67→    sent_at = Column(DateTime)
    68→    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow)
    69→
    70→    # Relationships
    71→    candidate = relationship("Candidate", back_populates="messages")
    72→
    73→    def __repr__(self):
    74→        return f"<Message(id={self.id}, candidate_id='{self.candidate_maimai_id}', status='{self.status}')>"
    75→
    76→
    77→class SearchHistory(Base):
    78→    """Search history model - tracks all searches performed."""
    79→
    80→    __tablename__ = 'search_history'
    81→
    82→    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    83→    criteria = Column(JSON)  # Search parameters as JSON
    84→    results_count = Column(Integer, default=0)
    85→    executed_at = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, index=True)
    86→
    87→    def __repr__(self):
    88→        return f"<SearchHistory(id={self.id}, results={self.results_count}, date='{self.executed_at}')>"
    89→
    90→
    91→class ActionLog(Base):
    92→    """Action log model - tracks all actions for rate limiting."""
    93→
    94→    __tablename__ = 'action_logs'
    95→
    96→    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    97→    action_type = Column(String(50), nullable=False)  # 'search', 'view_profile', 'send_message'
    98→    timestamp = Column(DateTime, default=_utcnow, nullable=False)
    99→    details = Column(JSON)  # Additional action details
   100→
   101→    # Composite index for efficient rate limiting queries
   102→    __table_args__ = (
   103→        Index('idx_action_logs_type_time', 'action_type', 'timestamp'),
   104→    )
   105→
   106→    def __repr__(self):
   107→        return f"<ActionLog(id={self.id}, type='{self.action_type}', time='{self.timestamp}')>"
   108→
   109→
   110→class DatabaseManager:
   111→    """Database manager for initialization and session management."""
   112→
   113→    def __init__(self, db_path: Optional[str] = None):
   114→        """Initialize database manager."""
   115→        if db_path is None:
   116→            config = get_config()
   117→            db_path = config.get('data.database_path', 'data/maimai.db')
   118→
   119→        # Ensure data directory exists
   120→        db_file = Path(db_path)
   121→        db_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   122→
   123→        # Create engine and session
   124→        # StaticPool uses a single connection, avoiding SQLite "database is locked" errors
   125→        self.db_path = db_path
   126→        self.engine = create_engine(
   127→            f'sqlite:///{db_path}',
   128→            echo=False,
   129→            poolclass=StaticPool,
   130→            connect_args={"check_same_thread": False},
   131→        )
   132→        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)
   133→
   134→        # Auto-create tables and run migrations
   135→        self.init_db()
   136→
   137→        logger.info(f"Database initialized at: {db_path}")
   138→
   139→    def init_db(self):
   140→        """Create all tables and run pending migrations."""
   141→        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
   142→        logger.info("Database tables created successfully")
   143→
   144→        from src.models.migrations import run_migrations
   145→        run_migrations(self.engine)
   146→
   147→    @contextmanager
   148→    def session_scope(self):
   149→        """Provide a transactional scope with auto commit/rollback/close."""
   150→        session = self.Session()
   151→        try:
   152→            yield session
   153→            session.commit()
   154→        except Exception:
   155→            session.rollback()
   156→            raise
   157→        finally:
   158→            session.close()
   159→
   160→    def drop_all(self):
   161→        """Drop all tables (use with caution!)."""
   162→        Base.metadata.drop_all(self.engine)
   163→        logger.warning("All database tables dropped")
   164→
   165→
   166→# Global database manager instance
   167→_db_manager = None
   168→
   169→
   170→def get_db_manager() -> DatabaseManager:
   171→    """Get global database manager instance."""
   172→    global _db_manager
   173→    if _db_manager is None:
   174→        _db_manager = DatabaseManager()
   175→    return _db_manager
   176→

1→"""Pipeline service for search-and-message orchestration.
     2→
     3→Extracted from CLI layer to separate business logic from argument parsing.
     4→Supports dual-path: search_center (Path A) and enterprise (Path B).
     5→"""
     6→
     7→from datetime import datetime

    10→from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple
    11→
    12→from rich.console import Console
    13→from rich.table import Table

    15→from src.core.browser import BrowserManager
    16→from src.modules.search import search_candidates, search_candidates_enterprise, CandidateBasic

    21→class SearchMode(str, Enum):
    22→    """Search mode for pipeline dispatch."""

    24→    ENTERPRISE = "enterprise"
    25→
    26→
    27→def _candidates_to_dicts(candidates: List[CandidateBasic]) -> List[dict]:
    28→    """Convert CandidateBasic objects to dicts for message batch functions."""
    29→    return [
    30→        {
    31→            'maimai_id': c.maimai_id,
    32→            'name': c.name,
    33→            'company': c.company,
    34→            'title': c.title,
    35→            'profile_url': c.profile_url,
    36→        }
    37→        for c in candidates
    38→    ]

    40→
    41→async def search_all_keywords(
    42→    browser: BrowserManager,
    43→    keywords: Tuple[str, ...],
    44→    company: Tuple[str, ...],
    45→    location: Optional[str],
    46→    max_pages: int,

    48→) -> Tuple[List[CandidateBasic], Set[str]]:
    49→    """Iterate keywords, deduplicate, return combined results.
    50→
    51→    Args:
    52→        browser: BrowserManager instance
    53→        keywords: Keyword strings to search
    54→        company: Company filter strings
    55→        location: Location filter or None
    56→        max_pages: Pages per keyword
    57→        start_page: First page number
    58→
    59→    Returns:
    60→        (deduplicated candidates list, set of seen IDs)
    61→    """
    62→    all_candidates: List[CandidateBasic] = []
    63→    seen_ids: Set[str] = set()
    64→
    65→    for idx, kw in enumerate(keywords):
    66→        console.print(f"\n[bold blue][{idx+1}/{len(keywords)}] Searching: {kw}[/bold blue]")
    67→
    68→        candidates = await search_candidates(
    69→            keywords=kw,
    70→            company=company,
    71→            location=location,
    72→            pages=max_pages,
    73→            start_page=start_page,
    74→            browser=browser,
    75→        )
    76→
    77→        new_count = 0
    78→        for c in candidates:
    79→            if c.maimai_id not in seen_ids:
    80→                seen_ids.add(c.maimai_id)
    81→                all_candidates.append(c)
    82→                new_count += 1
    83→
    84→        console.print(f"  Found {len(candidates)} candidates, {new_count} new (deduplicated)")
    85→
    86→    return all_candidates, seen_ids

1→"""Rate limiter for preventing account bans."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
     5→from typing import Tuple, Optional
     6→
     7→from loguru import logger
     8→from sqlalchemy import func
     9→
    10→from src.models.database import ActionLog, get_db_manager, DatabaseManager
    11→from src.utils.config import get_config, Config
    12→from src.exceptions.errors import RateLimitExceededError
    13→
    14→
    15→class RateLimiter:
    16→    """Rate limiter for controlling action frequency.
    17→
    18→    Supports adaptive throttling: when captchas or errors are reported,
    19→    limits are temporarily tightened to reduce detection risk.
    20→    """
    21→
    22→    def __init__(self, config: Optional[Config] = None, db_manager: Optional[DatabaseManager] = None):
    23→        """Initialize rate limiter with configuration.
    24→
    25→        Args:
    26→            config: Config instance (uses global singleton if None)
    27→            db_manager: DatabaseManager instance (uses global singleton if None)
    28→        """
    29→        self.config = config or get_config()
    30→        self.db_manager = db_manager or get_db_manager()
    31→
    32→        # Adaptive throttle state
    33→        self._throttle_factor: float = 1.0  # 1.0 = normal, <1.0 = stricter
    34→        self._captcha_timestamps: list = []
    35→        self._pruned: bool = False  # one-time prune flag
    36→
    37→    def report_captcha(self) -> None:
    38→        """Report a captcha occurrence to trigger adaptive throttling."""
    39→        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    40→        self._captcha_timestamps.append(now)
    41→        # Keep only last 30 minutes of captcha events
    42→        cutoff = now - timedelta(minutes=30)
    43→        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    44→        self._update_throttle_factor()
    45→
    46→    def _update_throttle_factor(self) -> None:
    47→        """Recalculate throttle factor based on recent captcha frequency.
    48→
    49→        Throttle factor ranges from 1.0 (normal) to 0.3 (heavily throttled).
    50→        - 1 captcha in 30min: reduce to 0.7x (30% fewer operations allowed)
    51→        - 2 captchas: reduce to 0.5x
    52→        - 3+ captchas: reduce to 0.3x
    53→        """
    54→        captcha_count = len(self._captcha_timestamps)
    55→
    56→        if captcha_count >= 3:
    57→            self._throttle_factor = 0.3
    58→        elif captcha_count >= 2:
    59→            self._throttle_factor = 0.5
    60→        elif captcha_count >= 1:
    61→            self._throttle_factor = 0.7
    62→        else:
    63→            self._throttle_factor = 1.0
    64→
    65→        if self._throttle_factor < 1.0:
    66→            logger.warning(
    67→                f"Adaptive throttle active: factor={self._throttle_factor:.1f} "
    68→                f"(captchas={captcha_count} in last 30min)"
    69→            )
    70→
    71→    def _refresh_throttle(self) -> None:
    72→        """Prune expired timestamps and recalculate throttle factor."""
    73→        cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=30)
    74→        self._captcha_timestamps = [t for t in self._captcha_timestamps if t >= cutoff]
    75→        self._update_throttle_factor()
    76→
    77→    @property
    78→    def throttle_factor(self) -> float:
    79→        """Current adaptive throttle factor (1.0 = normal, <1.0 = throttled)."""
    80→        return self._throttle_factor
    81→
    82→    def _get_limits(self, action_type: str) -> Tuple[int, int, int]:
    83→        """
    84→        Get rate limits for an action type, adjusted by adaptive throttle.
    85→
    86→        Args:
    87→            action_type: Type of action ('search', 'view_profile', 'send_message')
    88→
    89→        Returns:
    90→            Tuple of (per_hour_limit, per_day_limit, min_interval_seconds)
    91→        """
    92→        action_map = {
    93→            'search': ('search_per_hour', 'search_per_day'),
    94→            'view_profile': ('profile_view_per_hour', 'profile_view_per_day'),
    95→            'send_message': ('message_per_hour', 'message_per_day'),
    96→        }
    97→
    98→        if action_type not in action_map:
    99→            raise ValueError(f"Unknown action type: {action_type}")
   100→
   101→        hour_key, day_key = action_map[action_type]
   102→
   103→        per_hour = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{hour_key}', 20)
   104→        per_day = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{day_key}', 100)
   105→        min_interval = self.config.get('rate_limits.min_interval_seconds', 30)
   106→
   107→        # Apply adaptive throttle: reduce limits and increase interval
   108→        per_hour = max(1, int(per_hour * self._throttle_factor))
   109→        per_day = max(1, int(per_day * self._throttle_factor))
   110→        if self._throttle_factor < 1.0:
   111→            min_interval = int(min_interval / self._throttle_factor)
   112→
   113→        return per_hour, per_day, min_interval
   114→
   115→    def _count_actions(self, action_type: str, since: datetime) -> int:
   116→        """
   117→        Count actions of a type since a given time.
   118→
   119→        Args:
   120→            action_type: Type of action
   121→            since: Count actions since this time
   122→
   123→        Returns:
   124→            Number of actions
   125→        """
   126→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   127→            count = session.query(func.count(ActionLog.id)).filter(
   128→                ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   129→                ActionLog.timestamp >= since
   130→            ).scalar()
   131→
   132→            return count or 0
   133→
   134→    @staticmethod
   135→    def _ensure_aware(dt: Optional[datetime]) -> Optional[datetime]:
   136→        """Attach UTC timezone to naive datetimes returned by SQLite."""
   137→        if dt is not None and dt.tzinfo is None:
   138→            return dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
   139→        return dt
   140→
   141→    def _get_last_action_time(self, action_type: Optional[str] = None) -> Optional[datetime]:
   142→        """
   143→        Get timestamp of last action.
   144→
   145→        Args:
   146→            action_type: Type of action, or None for any action
   147→
   148→        Returns:
   149→            Timestamp of last action (timezone-aware), or None if no actions
   150→        """
   151→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   152→            query = session.query(func.max(ActionLog.timestamp))
   153→
   154→            if action_type:
   155→                query = query.filter(ActionLog.action_type == action_type)
   156→
   157→            last_time = query.scalar()
   158→            return self._ensure_aware(last_time)
   159→
   160→    def check_rate_limit(self, action_type: str) -> None:
   161→        """
   162→        Check if action is allowed under rate limits.
   163→
   164→        Args:
   165→            action_type: Type of action to check
   166→
   167→        Raises:
   168→            RateLimitExceededError: If rate limit is exceeded
   169→        """
   170→        # Prune stale entries once per session to keep counts accurate
   171→        if not self._pruned:
   172→            self.prune_old_entries()
   173→            self._pruned = True
   174→
   175→        self._refresh_throttle()
   176→        per_hour, per_day, min_interval = self._get_limits(action_type)
   177→
   178→        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   179→        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
   180→        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)
   181→
   182→        # Check hourly limit
   183→        hourly_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
   184→        if hourly_count >= per_hour:
   185→            # Calculate wait time until oldest action expires
   186→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   187→                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
   188→                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   189→                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_hour_ago
   190→                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()
   191→
   192→                if oldest_in_window:
   193→                    oldest_ts = self._ensure_aware(oldest_in_window[0])
   194→                    wait_until = oldest_ts + timedelta(hours=1)
   195→                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
   196→                else:
   197→                    wait_seconds = 3600
   198→
   199→            raise RateLimitExceededError(
   200→                action_type=action_type,
   201→                wait_time=wait_seconds,
   202→                message=f"Hourly rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
   203→                        f"Limit: {per_hour}/hour. Wait {wait_seconds // 60} minutes."
   204→            )
   205→
   206→        # Check daily limit
   207→        daily_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
   208→        if daily_count >= per_day:
   209→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   210→                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
   211→                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   212→                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_day_ago
   213→                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()
   214→
   215→                if oldest_in_window:
   216→                    oldest_ts = self._ensure_aware(oldest_in_window[0])
   217→                    wait_until = oldest_ts + timedelta(days=1)
   218→                    wait_seconds = max(1, int((wait_until - now).total_seconds()))
   219→                else:
   220→                    wait_seconds = 86400
   221→
   222→            raise RateLimitExceededError(
   223→                action_type=action_type,
   224→                wait_time=wait_seconds,
   225→                message=f"Daily rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
   226→                        f"Limit: {per_day}/day. Wait {wait_seconds // 3600} hours."
   227→            )
   228→
   229→        # Check minimum interval (per-action-type to avoid cross-type blocking)
   230→        last_action = self._get_last_action_time(action_type)
   231→        if last_action:
   232→            elapsed = (now - last_action).total_seconds()
   233→            if elapsed < min_interval:
   234→                wait_seconds = max(1, int(min_interval - elapsed))
   235→                raise RateLimitExceededError(
   236→                    action_type='any',
   237→                    wait_time=wait_seconds,
   238→                    message=f"Minimum interval not met. Wait {wait_seconds} seconds."
   239→                )
   240→
   241→        logger.debug(f"Rate limit check passed for {action_type}")
   242→
   243→    def log_action(self, action_type: str, details: Optional[dict] = None) -> None:
   244→        """
   245→        Log an action to the database.
   246→
   247→        Args:
   248→            action_type: Type of action
   249→            details: Optional action details
   250→        """
   251→        with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   252→            action_log = ActionLog(
   253→                action_type=action_type,
   254→                timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
   255→                details=details or {}
   256→            )
   257→            session.add(action_log)
   258→
   259→        logger.debug(f"Logged action: {action_type}")
   260→
   261→    def prune_old_entries(self, retention_hours: int = 48) -> int:
   262→        """Remove ActionLog entries older than the retention window.
   263→
   264→        This prevents stale counts from accumulating across sessions and
   265→        keeps the ActionLog table from growing indefinitely.
   266→
   267→        Args:
   268→            retention_hours: Delete entries older than this many hours (default 48)
   269→
   270→        Returns:
   271→            Number of entries deleted
   272→        """
   273→        cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=retention_hours)
   274→        try:
   275→            with self.db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   276→                count = session.query(ActionLog).filter(
   277→                    ActionLog.timestamp < cutoff
   278→                ).delete()
   279→                logger.debug(f"Pruned {count} old ActionLog entries (older than {retention_hours}h)")
   280→                return count
   281→        except Exception as e:
   282→            logger.error(f"Failed to prune ActionLog entries: {e}")
   283→            return 0
   284→
   285→    def get_quota_status(self, action_type: str) -> dict:
   286→        """
   287→        Get current quota status for an action type.
   288→
   289→        Args:
   290→            action_type: Type of action
   291→
   292→        Returns:
   293→            Dictionary with quota information
   294→        """
   295→        per_hour, per_day, _ = self._get_limits(action_type)
   296→
   297→        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   298→        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
   299→        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)
   300→
   301→        hourly_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
   302→        daily_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
   303→
   304→        return {
   305→            'action_type': action_type,
   306→            'hourly': {
   307→                'used': hourly_used,
   308→                'limit': per_hour,
   309→                'remaining': max(0, per_hour - hourly_used)
   310→            },
   311→            'daily': {
   312→                'used': daily_used,
   313→                'limit': per_day,
   314→                'remaining': max(0, per_day - daily_used)
   315→            }
   316→        }
   317→
   318→
   319→async def async_check_rate_limit(
   320→    action_type: str,
   321→    max_retries: int = 60,
   322→    max_total_wait: int = 1800,
   323→) -> None:
   324→    """
   325→    Check rate limit, waiting through any violations until the action is allowed.
   326→
   327→    Blocks until the rate limit clears. Raises RateLimitExceededError if
   328→    still blocked after max_retries attempts or max_total_wait seconds
   329→    (prevents hanging for hours when daily limits are hit).
   330→
   331→    Args:
   332→        action_type: Type of action to check
   333→        max_retries: Maximum wait-retry cycles before giving up
   334→        max_total_wait: Maximum total seconds to wait before giving up (default 30 min)
   335→    """
   336→    import time
   337→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
   338→    start = time.monotonic()
   339→    for attempt in range(max_retries):
   340→        try:
   341→            rate_limiter.check_rate_limit(action_type)
   342→            return
   343→        except RateLimitExceededError as e:
   344→            elapsed = time.monotonic() - start
   345→            if attempt >= max_retries - 1 or elapsed + e.wait_time > max_total_wait:
   346→                raise
   347→            wait = e.wait_time + 1  # +1s buffer
   348→            logger.info(f"Rate limit hit: {e}. Waiting {wait}s before retrying (attempt {attempt + 1}/{max_retries}, elapsed {elapsed:.0f}s)...")
   349→            await asyncio.sleep(wait)
   350→
   351→
   352→# Global rate limiter instance
   353→_rate_limiter = None
   354→
   355→
   356→def get_rate_limiter() -> RateLimiter:
   357→    """Get global rate limiter instance."""
   358→    global _rate_limiter
   359→    if _rate_limiter is None:
   360→        _rate_limiter = RateLimiter()
   361→    return _rate_limiter
   362→

Thoroughly explore this Maimai recruitment assistant project. I need to understand:

1. The CLI layer - how commands are structured in src/cli/main.py, what Click commands exist, how they call into feature modules
2. The core infrastructure - browser.py singleton pattern, how async is handled (run_async), session management
3. Feature modules - how search, message, profile, pipeline work, their async patterns

   215→def generate_pipeline_report(
   216→    candidates: List[CandidateBasic],
   217→    results: dict,
   218→    keywords: Tuple[str, ...],
   219→    template: str,
   220→    mode: SearchMode,
   221→) -> str:
   222→    """Generate an Excel report for a pipeline run.
   223→
   224→    Args:
   225→        candidates: All candidates from search
   226→        results: Send results dict (total/sent/failed/skipped/rate_limited/details)
   227→        keywords: Search keywords used
   228→        template: Template name used
   229→        mode: Search mode (search_center or enterprise)
   230→
   231→    Returns:
   232→        Path to the generated report file
   233→    """
   234→    from openpyxl import Workbook
   235→    from openpyxl.styles import Font, PatternFill, Alignment, Border, Side
   236→    from loguru import logger
   237→
   238→    from src.utils.config import get_config
   239→    timestamp = datetime.now()
   240→    config = get_config()
   241→    report_dir = Path(config.get('data.report_dir', 'data/reports'))
   242→    report_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   243→    report_path = report_dir / f"pipeline_{timestamp.strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')}.xlsx"
   244→
   245→    wb = Workbook()
   246→
   247→    # --- Sheet 1: Overview ---
   248→    ws_overview = wb.active
   249→    ws_overview.title = "概览"
   250→
   251→    header_font = Font(bold=True, color="FFFFFF")
   252→    header_fill = PatternFill(start_color="4472C4", end_color="4472C4", fill_type="solid")
   253→    border = Border(
   254→        left=Side(style="thin"), right=Side(style="thin"),
   255→        top=Side(style="thin"), bottom=Side(style="thin"),
   256→    )
   257→
   258→    overview_rows = [
   259→        ("执行时间", timestamp.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")),
   260→        ("搜索关键词", ", ".join(keywords)),
   261→        ("消息模板", template),
   262→        ("搜索模式", mode.value),
   263→        ("搜索结果数", results["total"]),
   264→        ("发送总数", results["sent"] + results["failed"]),
   265→        ("成功", results["sent"]),
   266→        ("失败", results["failed"]),
   267→        ("跳过(已沟通)", results["skipped"]),
   268→        ("限流", results["rate_limited"]),
   269→    ]
   270→
   271→    for r_idx, (key, value) in enumerate(overview_rows, 1):
   272→        cell_key = ws_overview.cell(row=r_idx, column=1, value=key)
   273→        cell_val = ws_overview.cell(row=r_idx, column=2, value=value)
   274→        cell_key.font = Font(bold=True)
   275→        for cell in (cell_key, cell_val):
   276→            cell.border = border
   277→            cell.alignment = Alignment(vertical="center")
   278→
   279→    ws_overview.column_dimensions["A"].width = 18
   280→    ws_overview.column_dimensions["B"].width = 40
   281→
   282→    # --- Sheet 2: Candidate Details ---
   283→    ws_details = wb.create_sheet(title="候选人详情")
   284→
   285→    columns = ["序号", "姓名", "职位", "公司", "地区", "脉脉ID", "沟通状态", "失败原因"]
   286→    for c_idx, col_name in enumerate(columns, 1):
   287→        cell = ws_details.cell(row=1, column=c_idx, value=col_name)
   288→        cell.font = header_font
   289→        cell.fill = header_fill
   290→        cell.alignment = Alignment(horizontal="center", vertical="center")
   291→        cell.border = border
   292→
   293→    # Build a lookup from maimai_id -> detail
   294→    details_by_id: Dict[str, dict] = {}
   295→    for detail in results.get("details", []):
   296→        details_by_id[detail["maimai_id"]] = detail
   297→
   298→    status_map = {
   299→        "sent": "已发送",
   300→        "failed": "失败",
   301→        "skipped": "跳过",
   302→        "rate_limited": "限流",
   303→    }
   304→
   305→    for r_idx, c in enumerate(candidates, 1):
   306→        detail = details_by_id.get(c.maimai_id, {})
   307→        raw_status = detail.get("status", "not_attempted")
   308→        status_label = status_map.get(raw_status, "未处理")
   309→        reason = detail.get("reason", "")
   310→
   311→        row_data = [r_idx, c.name, c.title, c.company, c.location or "", c.maimai_id, status_label, reason]
   312→        for c_idx, value in enumerate(row_data, 1):
   313→            cell = ws_details.cell(row=r_idx + 1, column=c_idx, value=value)
   314→            cell.border = border
   315→            cell.alignment = Alignment(vertical="center", wrap_text=True)
   316→
   317→    detail_widths = {"A": 6, "B": 12, "C": 25, "D": 20, "E": 12, "F": 18, "G": 12, "H": 30}
   318→    for col, width in detail_widths.items():
   319→        ws_details.column_dimensions[col].width = width
   320→
   321→    ws_details.freeze_panes = "A2"
   322→    ws_details.auto_filter.ref = ws_details.dimensions
   323→
   324→    wb.save(str(report_path))
   325→    logger.info(f"Pipeline report saved to: {report_path}")
   326→    console.print(f"[green]Pipeline report saved to: {report_path}[/green]")
   327→    return str(report_path)

   330→def display_candidates_table(candidates: List[CandidateBasic], title: str) -> None:
   331→    """Display a Rich table of candidates."""
   332→    table = Table(title=f"{title} ({len(candidates)} candidates)")
   333→    table.add_column("#", style="dim", justify="right")
   334→    table.add_column("Name", style="cyan")
   335→    table.add_column("Title", style="green")
   336→    table.add_column("Company", style="yellow")
   337→    table.add_column("Location", style="blue")
   338→    has_scores = any(getattr(c, 'score', None) is not None for c in candidates[:1])
   339→    if has_scores:
   340→        table.add_column("Score", style="magenta", justify="right")
   341→    table.add_column("ID", style="dim")
   342→
   343→    for i, c in enumerate(candidates[:50], 1):
   344→        row = [str(i), c.name, c.title, c.company, c.location or '']
   345→        if has_scores:
   346→            row.append(str(getattr(c, 'score', '')))
   347→        row.append(c.maimai_id)
   348→        table.add_row(*row)
   349→
   350→    console.print(table)
   351→
   352→    if len(candidates) > 50:
   353→        console.print(f"[dim]... and {len(candidates) - 50} more[/dim]")

   374→async def run_pipeline_default(
   375→    browser: BrowserManager,
   376→    keywords: Tuple[str, ...],
   377→    company: Tuple[str, ...],
   378→    location: Optional[str],
   379→    max_pages: int,
   380→    template: str,
   381→    variables: Dict[str, str],
   382→    dry_run: bool,
   383→    skip_messaged: bool,
   384→    interactive: bool = False,
   385→    mode: SearchMode = SearchMode.SEARCH_CENTER,
   386→    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
   387→) -> None:
   388→    """Pipeline: search all pages first, then send messages.
   389→
   390→    Saves a checkpoint after search completes so that message sending
   391→    can be resumed if interrupted.
   392→    """
   393→    from src.utils.checkpoint import CheckpointManager
   394→
   395→    checkpoint = CheckpointManager()
   396→
   397→    if mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE:
   398→        # Keep enterprise tab open for inline messaging (unless dry_run)
   399→        all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords_enterprise(
   400→            browser, keywords, company, max_pages,
   401→            keep_last_tab_open=not dry_run,
   402→        )
   403→    else:
   404→        all_candidates, seen_ids = await search_all_keywords(
   405→            browser, keywords, company, location, max_pages,
   406→        )
   407→
   408→    if not all_candidates:
   409→        console.print("[yellow]No candidates found for any keyword.[/yellow]")
   410→        return
   411→
   412→    # Save checkpoint after search (batch_pages=0 marks non-batch mode)
   413→    _save_checkpoint(
   414→        checkpoint, keywords, template, variables, company, location,
   415→        max_pages, 0, skip_messaged, interactive,
   416→        max_pages, [], seen_ids, {'total': 0, 'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'skipped': 0, 'rate_limited': 0},
   417→        mode=mode.value, dry_run=dry_run,
   418→    )
   419→
   420→    display_candidates_table(all_candidates, "Pipeline Results")
   421→
   422→    if dry_run:
   423→        console.print(f"\n[cyan][Dry Run] {len(all_candidates)} candidates found. No messages sent.[/cyan]")
   424→        checkpoint.delete()
   425→        return
   426→
   427→    if mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE:
   428→        results = await send_batch_enterprise(
   429→            all_candidates, template, variables, list(keywords), browser, skip_messaged,
   430→            skip_confirmation=skip_confirmation,
   431→        )
   432→    else:
   433→        results = await send_batch(
   434→            all_candidates, template, variables, list(keywords), browser, skip_messaged, interactive,
   435→            skip_confirmation=skip_confirmation,
   436→        )
   437→
   438→    # Fetch final quota for summary display
   439→    from src.modules.message import fetch_contact_quota
   440→    quota = await fetch_contact_quota(browser)
   441→    display_send_results(results, quota=quota)
   442→    generate_pipeline_report(all_candidates, results, keywords, template, mode)
   443→
   444→    # Clean up checkpoint on successful completion
   445→    checkpoint.delete()
   446→    console.print("[dim]Checkpoint cleared (pipeline complete)[/dim]")
   447→

   449→async def run_pipeline_batched(
   450→    browser: BrowserManager,
   451→    keywords: Tuple[str, ...],
   452→    company: Tuple[str, ...],
   453→    location: Optional[str],
   454→    max_pages: int,
   455→    batch_pages: int,
   456→    template: str,
   457→    variables: Dict[str, str],
   458→    dry_run: bool,
   459→    skip_messaged: bool,
   460→    interactive: bool = False,
   461→    resume_ckpt=None,
   462→    mode: SearchMode = SearchMode.SEARCH_CENTER,
   463→    skip_confirmation: bool = False,
   464→) -> None:
   465→    """Batched pipeline: search N pages, send messages, repeat."""
   466→    from src.modules.auth import ensure_logged_in, check_login_status
   467→    from src.core.session import get_session_manager
   468→    from src.utils.checkpoint import CheckpointManager
   469→
   470→    # Restore state from checkpoint or start fresh
   471→    if resume_ckpt:
   472→        # On resume, only treat sent_ids as truly "seen" (skip-worthy).
   473→        # Candidates that were found but not sent should be retried.
   474→        sent_ids = list(resume_ckpt.sent_ids)
   475→        seen_ids = set(sent_ids)  # only skip candidates already successfully sent
   476→        total_results = resume_ckpt.total_results or {
   477→            'total': 0, 'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'skipped': 0, 'rate_limited': 0,
   478→        }
   479→        start_from_page = resume_ckpt.completed_pages + 1
   480→        console.print(f"[cyan]Resuming from page {start_from_page} (sent: {len(sent_ids)}, retrying unsent)[/cyan]")
   481→        checkpoint = resume_ckpt
   482→    else:
   483→        seen_ids = set()
   484→        sent_ids = []
   485→        total_results = {'total': 0, 'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'skipped': 0, 'rate_limited': 0}
   486→        start_from_page = 1
   487→        checkpoint = CheckpointManager()
   488→
   489→    all_candidates: List[CandidateBasic] = []
   490→    all_details: List[dict] = []
   491→    batch_num = 0
   492→    consecutive_empty_batches = 0
   493→
   494→    batch_start = start_from_page
   495→    while batch_start <= max_pages:
   496→        batch_page_count = min(batch_pages, max_pages - batch_start + 1)
   497→        batch_num += 1
   498→        batch_end = batch_start + batch_page_count - 1
   499→
   500→        console.print(f"\n[bold magenta]━━━ Batch {batch_num}: Pages {batch_start}-{batch_end} ━━━[/bold magenta]")
   501→
   502→        if mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE:
   503→            batch_candidates, _ = await search_all_keywords_enterprise(
   504→                browser, keywords, company, batch_page_count,
   505→                keep_last_tab_open=not dry_run,
   506→            )
   507→        else:
   508→            batch_candidates, _ = await search_all_keywords(
   509→                browser, keywords, company, location, batch_page_count, start_page=batch_start,
   510→            )
   511→        # Filter out globally seen IDs
   512→        batch_candidates = [c for c in batch_candidates if c.maimai_id not in seen_ids]
   513→        seen_ids.update(c.maimai_id for c in batch_candidates)
   514→        all_candidates.extend(batch_candidates)
   515→
   516→        if not batch_candidates:
   517→            consecutive_empty_batches += 1
   518→            console.print(f"[yellow]No new candidates in batch {batch_num}.[/yellow]")
   519→
   520→            if consecutive_empty_batches >= 2:
   521→                console.print("[red]Multiple consecutive empty batches detected. Verifying login status...[/red]")
   522→                if not await check_login_status(browser.page):
   523→                    console.print("[red]Session expired! Clearing session and re-authenticating...[/red]")
   524→                    session_manager = get_session_manager()
   525→                    await session_manager.clear()
   526→                    await ensure_logged_in(browser)
   527→                    consecutive_empty_batches = 0
   528→                    console.print("[green]Re-authenticated. Retrying current batch...[/green]")
   529→                    batch_num -= 1
   530→                    continue
   531→                else:
   532→                    console.print("[yellow]Session appears valid. Possibly no more results for these keywords.[/yellow]")
   533→
   534→            _save_checkpoint(
   535→                checkpoint, keywords, template, variables, company, location,
   536→                max_pages, batch_pages, skip_messaged, interactive,
   537→                batch_end, sent_ids, seen_ids, total_results,
   538→                mode=mode.value, dry_run=dry_run,
   539→            )
   540→            batch_start += batch_pages
   541→            continue
   542→        else:
   543→            consecutive_empty_batches = 0
   544→
   545→        display_candidates_table(batch_candidates, f"Batch {batch_num} Results")
   546→
   547→        if dry_run:
   548→            console.print(f"\n[cyan][Dry Run] Batch {batch_num}: {len(batch_candidates)} candidates found. No messages sent.[/cyan]")
   549→            _save_checkpoint(
   550→                checkpoint, keywords, template, variables, company, location,
   551→                max_pages, batch_pages, skip_messaged, interactive,
   552→                batch_end, sent_ids, seen_ids, total_results,
   553→                mode=mode.value, dry_run=dry_run,
   554→            )
   555→            batch_start += batch_pages
   556→            continue
   557→
   558→        if mode == SearchMode.ENTERPRISE:
   559→            results = await send_batch_enterprise(
   560→                batch_candidates, template, variables, list(keywords), browser, skip_messaged,
   561→                skip_confirmation=skip_confirmation,
   562→            )
   563→        else:
   564→            results = await send_batch(
   565→                batch_candidates, template, variables, list(keywords), browser, skip_messaged, interactive,
   566→                skip_confirmation=skip_confirmation,
   567→            )
   568→        from src.modules.message import fetch_contact_quota
   569→        batch_quota = await fetch_contact_quota(browser)
   570→        display_send_results(results, title=f"Batch {batch_num} Send Results", quota=batch_quota)
   571→
   572→        all_details.extend(results.get('details', []))
   573→        for detail in results.get('details', []):
   574→            if detail.get('status') == 'sent':
   575→                sent_ids.append(detail['maimai_id'])
   576→
   577→        for key in total_results:
   578→            total_results[key] += results[key]
   579→
   580→        _save_checkpoint(
   581→            checkpoint, keywords, template, variables, company, location,
   582→            max_pages, batch_pages, skip_messaged, interactive,
   583→            batch_end, sent_ids, seen_ids, total_results,
   584→            mode=mode.value, dry_run=dry_run,
   585→        )
   586→        console.print(f"[dim]Checkpoint saved (page {batch_end})[/dim]")
   587→
   588→        batch_start += batch_pages
   589→
   590→    # Final summary
   591→    console.print(f"\n[bold magenta]━━━ Pipeline Complete ━━━[/bold magenta]")
   592→    console.print(f"Total unique candidates found: {len(seen_ids)}")
   593→    if not dry_run and total_results['total'] > 0:
   594→        from src.modules.message import fetch_contact_quota
   595→        final_quota = await fetch_contact_quota(browser)
   596→        display_send_results(total_results, title="Overall Send Results", quota=final_quota)
   597→        total_results_with_details = {**total_results, 'details': all_details}
   598→        generate_pipeline_report(all_candidates, total_results_with_details, keywords, template, mode)
   599→
   600→    checkpoint.delete()
   601→    console.print("[dim]Checkpoint cleared (pipeline complete)[/dim]")

   604→def _save_checkpoint(
   605→    checkpoint, keywords, template, variables, company, location,
   606→    max_pages, batch_pages, skip_messaged, interactive,
   607→    completed_pages, sent_ids, seen_ids, total_results,
   608→    mode: str = "search_center",
   609→    dry_run: bool = False,
   610→) -> None:
   611→    """Save pipeline checkpoint (DRY helper)."""
   612→    checkpoint.save(
   613→        keywords=keywords, template=template, variables=variables,
   614→        company=company, location=location, max_pages=max_pages,
   615→        batch_pages=batch_pages, skip_messaged=skip_messaged,
   616→        interactive=interactive, completed_pages=completed_pages,
   617→        sent_ids=sent_ids, seen_ids=list(seen_ids),
   618→        total_results=total_results, mode=mode, dry_run=dry_run,
   619→    )
   620→

1→"""Authentication module for Maimai login.
     2→
     3→In CDP mode, the user logs in manually in their own Chrome before running
     4→CLI commands. This module verifies login status and provides guidance
     5→if the user is not logged in.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import asyncio
     9→from typing import Optional
    10→
    11→from patchright.async_api import Page
    12→from loguru import logger
    13→from rich.console import Console
    14→from rich.panel import Panel
    15→
    16→from src.core.browser import BrowserManager, get_browser_manager
    17→from src.utils.captcha import check_captcha
    18→from src.exceptions.errors import SessionExpiredError
    19→
    20→console = Console()
    21→
    22→
    23→async def login(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> bool:
    24→    """
    25→    Verify login status in CDP-connected Chrome.
    26→
    27→    In CDP mode, the user is expected to already be logged in.
    28→    If not, instructions are printed for manual login in the browser.
    29→
    30→    Args:
    31→        browser: Browser manager instance (uses global if None)
    32→
    33→    Returns:
    34→        True if logged in
    35→    """
    36→    if browser is None:
    37→        browser = get_browser_manager()
    38→
    39→    # Connect to Chrome via CDP
    40→    page = await browser.start()
    41→
    42→    # Check if already logged in
    43→    if await check_login_status(page):
    44→        console.print("[green]Already logged in![/green]")
    45→        # Save session as backup
    46→        await browser.save_session()
    47→        return True
    48→
    49→    # Not logged in — guide the user
    50→    console.print(Panel(
    51→        "[bold]Not Logged In[/bold]\n\n"
    52→        "Please log in to Maimai in your Chrome browser:\n"
    53→        "1. Go to https://maimai.cn/login in Chrome\n"
    54→        "2. Scan QR code with Maimai app, OR\n"
    55→        "3. Enter phone number and verification code\n\n"
    56→        "[dim]Then run this command again to verify.[/dim]",
    57→        title="Maimai Login",
    58→        border_style="cyan"
    59→    ))
    60→
    61→    # Poll for login (user may log in while we wait)
    62→    max_wait_seconds = 300  # 5 minutes timeout
    63→    poll_interval = 3
    64→    elapsed = 0
    65→
    66→    console.print("[dim]Waiting for login... (timeout: 5 minutes)[/dim]")
    67→
    68→    while elapsed < max_wait_seconds:
    69→        await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval)
    70→        elapsed += poll_interval
    71→
    72→        # Check for captcha
    73→        await check_captcha(page)
    74→
    75→        if await check_login_status(page):
    76→            console.print("[green]Login successful![/green]")
    77→            await browser.save_session()
    78→            console.print("[green]Session saved as backup[/green]")
    79→            return True
    80→

1→"""Anti-detection module for browser automation.
     2→
     3→Implements human-like interaction via Emunium + Patchright:
     4→- Human-like scrolling behavior (chunked, variable speed)
     5→- Human-like clicking via Emunium (natural mouse movement)
     6→- Human-like typing via Emunium (variable keystroke timing)
     7→
     8→Note: Webdriver property hiding and navigator masking are handled
     9→by Patchright automatically in CDP mode.
    10→"""
    11→
    12→import random
    13→
    14→from patchright.async_api import Page
    15→from emunium import EmuniumPlaywright
    16→
    17→from src.exceptions.errors import SelectorNotFoundError
    18→
    19→
    20→async def human_scroll(page: Page, direction: str = 'down', distance: int = None) -> None:
    21→    """
    22→    Perform human-like scrolling with variable speed.
    23→
    24→    Args:
    25→        page: Playwright page instance
    26→        direction: 'up' or 'down'
    27→        distance: Scroll distance in pixels (random if None)
    28→    """
    29→    if direction not in ('up', 'down'):
    30→        raise ValueError(f"Invalid scroll direction: {direction!r}, expected 'up' or 'down'")
    31→
    32→    if distance is None:
    33→        distance = random.randint(200, 600)
    34→
    35→    if direction == 'up':
    36→        distance = -distance
    37→
    38→    # Break scroll into smaller chunks
    39→    chunks = random.randint(3, 8)
    40→    chunk_size = distance // chunks
    41→
    42→    for i in range(chunks):
    43→        # Add variance to each chunk
    44→        actual_chunk = chunk_size + random.randint(-20, 20)
    45→
    46→        await page.mouse.wheel(0, actual_chunk)
    47→
    48→        # Variable delay between scroll chunks
    49→        delay = random.uniform(0.05, 0.15)
    50→        await page.wait_for_timeout(delay * 1000)
    51→
    52→    # Small pause after scrolling (human would look at content)
    53→    await page.wait_for_timeout(random.uniform(0.2, 0.5) * 1000)
    54→
    55→
    56→async def human_click(page: Page, selector: str) -> None:
    57→    """
    58→    Click an element with human-like behavior using Emunium.
    59→
    60→    Emunium provides natural mouse movement and click simulation.
    61→
    62→    Args:
    63→        page: Playwright page instance
    64→        selector: CSS selector for element to click
    65→    """
    66→    try:
    67→        element = await page.wait_for_selector(selector, timeout=10000)
    68→    except Exception as e:
    69→        if "timeout" in str(type(e).__name__).lower() or "timeout" in str(e).lower():
    70→            raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element not found: {selector}")
    71→        raise
    72→
    73→    if element is None:
    74→        raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element resolved to None: {selector}")
    75→
    76→    emunium = EmuniumPlaywright(page)
    77→    await emunium.click_at(element)
    78→
    79→
    80→async def human_type(page: Page, selector: str, text: str) -> None:
    81→    """
    82→    Type text with human-like timing using Emunium.
    83→
    84→    Args:
    85→        page: Playwright page instance
    86→        selector: CSS selector for input element
    87→        text: Text to type
    88→    """
    89→    try:
    90→        element = await page.wait_for_selector(selector, timeout=10000)
    91→    except Exception as e:
    92→        if "timeout" in str(type(e).__name__).lower() or "timeout" in str(e).lower():
    93→            raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element not found: {selector}")
    94→        raise
    95→
    96→    if element is None:
    97→        raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element resolved to None: {selector}")
    98→
    99→    emunium = EmuniumPlaywright(page)
   100→    await emunium.type_at(element, text)
